title: Vision Verbs
description: Master xem, ngắm, nhìn, thấy, trông, gặp — Vietnamese has 6+ ways to say 'see/look/watch'
intro: English uses 'see', 'look', 'watch' loosely. Vietnamese is very specific: 'xem' = watch/read for entertainment, 'ngắm' = gaze at something beautiful, 'nhìn' = look at with attention, 'thấy' = see (ability), 'trông' = look (appearance/judgment), 'gặp' = meet (see + talk to).

questions.explanation: 'xem' = watch for entertainment. 'xem ti vi' = watch TV, 'xem phim' = watch a movie.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'gaze at' something beautiful or a target?
questions.explanation: 'ngắm' = gaze at with appreciation. 'ngắm hoa' = look at flowers, 'ngắm trăng' = gaze at the moon.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'look at' with attention?
questions.explanation: 'nhìn' = look at, pay attention. 'Mark nhìn đồng hồ' = Mark looked at his watch.

questions.prompt: Ba tôi rất thích ___ bóng đá. (My dad loves to WATCH soccer.)
questions.explanation: 'xem' for watching entertainment/sports. 'xem bóng đá' = watch soccer.

questions.prompt: Họ đi ra vườn để ___ trăng. (They went to the garden to GAZE AT the moon.)
questions.explanation: 'ngắm' = gaze at something beautiful. The moon is admired aesthetically → 'ngắm trăng'.

questions.prompt: Gần đây mắt tôi không còn ___ rõ nữa. (Recently I can't SEE clearly anymore.)
questions.explanation: 'thấy' = ability to see. 'không thấy rõ' = can't see clearly. About visual ability, not deliberate looking.

questions.prompt: 'Trông cô ấy có vẻ vui' means:
questions.explanation: 'trông...có vẻ' = looks like / appears to be. Making a judgment about someone's appearance.

questions.prompt: Tôi ___ anh ấy trên phố và chúng tôi đi ăn trưa. (I MET him on the street and we had lunch.)
questions.explanation: 'gặp' = meet (see + talk to). Not just seeing, but having an encounter.

questions.prompt: 'xem' can also mean 'read'. In what context?
questions.correct: Reading for fun or information (books, newspapers)
options: Reading for fun or information (books, newspapers)
questions.explanation: 'Anh đã xem quyển sách mới chưa?' = Have you read the new book? 'xem' for casual reading.

questions.prompt: Màu này ___ ghê quá. (This color LOOKS awful.)
questions.explanation: 'trông' for making a judgment about appearance. 'trông ghê' = looks awful.

questions.prompt: Chị có thể ___ nhà giùm khi tôi đi vắng không? (Can you LOOK AFTER the house while I'm away?)
questions.explanation: 'trông' also means 'to keep an eye on / look after'. 'trông nhà' = watch the house, 'trông em bé' = babysit.

questions.prompt: In Southern Vietnamese, 'can see' is expressed as:
questions.explanation: South: 'nhìn thấy'. North: 'trông thấy'. Both mean 'to be able to see'. Regional difference!

questions.prompt: Cửa sổ ___ ra vườn. (The windows FACE the garden.)
questions.explanation: 'trông ra' (North) / 'nhìn ra' (South) = face towards. Describes which direction something faces.

questions.prompt: Anh Minh say sưa ___ đứa con trai đang ngủ. (Minh gazed adoringly at his sleeping son.)
questions.explanation: 'ngắm' for looking at something/someone with love and admiration. A father gazing at his child.

questions.prompt: Tôi hy vọng chúng ta có thể ___ nhau một lần nữa. (I hope we can MEET again.)
questions.explanation: 'gặp nhau' = meet each other. About having an encounter, not just seeing.

questions.prompt: Which is WRONG: 'Xin hãy xem đây' or 'Xin hãy nhìn đây'?
questions.correct: 'Xin hãy xem đây' — should be 'nhìn đây' (look here)
options: 'Xin hãy xem đây' — should be 'nhìn đây' (look here)
questions.explanation: 'nhìn đây' = look here (pay attention). 'xem' is for entertainment/reading, not for directing attention.
